claims.text: The Apollo space flight program ran from 1961 to 1972, with Apollo 17 the last manned mission; Apollo 18, 19, and 20 were planned but cancelled.

evidence.quote:
Apollo space flight program lasted
from 1961 to 1972. Apollo 11 was Neil
Armstrong's famous First Step Apollo 17
was the last manned mission to the Moon
in all 20 Apollo flights were plann

claims.rationale: Apollo ran 1961-1972. Apollo 17 (Dec 1972) was the last crewed lunar mission. Apollo 18-20 were cancelled in 1970 due to budget constraints — all well-documented NASA history.

20th 1969 when Neil Armstrong famously
said it's one small step for man

claims.rationale: July 20, 1969 lunar landing is among the most documented events in human history — TV broadcasts, telemetry, returned samples, independent tracking by multiple countries.

claims.text: In 2007 a YouTube user with the handle 'retired AFB' posted a video that claimed to show the interior of an Apollo spacecraft from a secret Apollo 20 mission.

this in 2007 a YouTube user with the

Apollo

claims.rationale: The 'retired AFB' Apollo 20 video series is a real documented 2007 YouTube upload that triggered the Apollo 20 hoax-legend cycle. The historical fact of posting is uncontested; the contents are fiction (see c_0011).

claims.text: 'Retired AFB' identified himself as William Rutledge of the US Air Force, the claimed mission commander of Apollo 20.

evidence.quote:
er lanoff and Rutledge soon after
releasing the video retired AFB admitted
he was William Rutledge of the United
States Air Force and Mission commander
of Apollo 20 which

claims.rationale: The claim of identity is what 'retired AFB' asserted online. No person by this name appears in NASA or USAF astronaut rosters — later shown to be an alias used by the French hoaxer Thierry Speth.

claims.text: Apollo 20's alleged mission was to identify and breach a two-mile-long spaceship that had supposedly crashed on the moon 1.5 billion years ago.

evidence.quote:
20's Mission identify and
if possible breach the two mile long
spaceship that crashed on the moon 1.5
billion years ago
[Music]
over time Commander R

claims.rationale: This is the fictional premise of the Apollo 20 hoax. A two-mile spaceship on the Moon would be trivially visible to every astronomer and orbiting telescope — it does not exist.

claims.text: The Apollo-Soyuz test flight was the first joint US-USSR space mission, commanded by cosmonaut Alexei Leonov on the Soviet side.

evidence.quote:

Alexei leonov commander

claims.rationale: The Apollo-Soyuz Test Project of July 1975 — the first US-USSR joint crewed spaceflight, with Leonov commanding Soyuz 19 — is well-documented history.

claims.text: Apollo 20 supposedly launched on August 16, 1976 aboard a Saturn V rocket in the dead of night on a secret joint mission.

evidence.quote:
eona Snyder
who would serve as the pilot then on
August 16 1976 in the dead of night a
Saturn V rocket took to the sky and
embarked on the most important and mo

claims.rationale: A Saturn V launch on August 16, 1976 would have been trivially observable from dozens of tracking networks worldwide; none occurred. The last Saturn V launch was Skylab's in May 1973.

claims.text: Former Air Force technician Carl Wolfe testified that while working on the 1965-66 Lunar Orbiter project at NASA Langley, a colleague showed him photographs of 'a base on the back side of the moon' with mushroom-shaped buildings and towers.

evidence.quote:

we've discovered a base on
the back side of the moon

mushroom-shaped buildings s

claims.rationale: Carl Wolfe's 2001 Disclosure Project testimony is real and on the record. His identity and job history check out. His testimony about what a colleague allegedly told him is uncorroborated.

claims.text: French artist Thierry Speth openly admitted creating the Apollo 20 videos as part of a science-fiction film project using practical effects and 3D CGI.

evidence.quote:
ed to creating them the videos
were created by French artists Thierry
speth as part of a science fiction film
Project he was working on he used a
combination of practical effects and 3D
to

claims.rationale: Speth's authorship of the Apollo 20 hoax videos is well documented — he eventually gave interviews confirming the art project origin and released related material including a novel.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] the videos were not leaked astronaut footage but artwork by a named French creator.
[debunks] 'William Rutledge' was Speth's online persona, not a real USAF astronaut.
[debunks] the two-mile spaceship story is part of Speth's fiction.
[debunks] no August-1976 Saturn V launch occurred; the narrative is invented.

claims.text: Chad Baxter finally interviewed Thierry Speth in 2016 — nine years after the videos appeared — and Speth confirmed authorship and showed the physical alien-woman mask.

claims.rationale: Baxter's 2016 interview with Speth is public and confirms origin of the Apollo 20 images; includes the alien-mask reveal.

claims.text: The entire Apollo 20 story is a hoax created by Thierry Speth, and the videos can be debunked frame by frame.

it there are a few articles out there

claims.rationale: Host's explicit verdict: 'absolutely no damn it' — the Apollo 20 story is not real.

claims.text: NASA's 2022 budget was $22 billion — a 90% reduction versus the 1970s Apollo-era budget — while the US Department of Defense budget was $722 billion.

evidence.quote:
n today's money
that war would cost a trillion dollars
in 2022 NASA's budget was 22 billion
dollars that's a budget reduction of 90
percent since the 1970s yet the United
Stat

claims.rationale: NASA's FY2022 budget of ~$24B and DoD budget of ~$700B+ are publicly available. The '90% reduction vs 1970s' claim requires inflation-adjusted comparison: Apollo peaked at ~4.4% of federal outlay vs ~0.5% in 2022, so the relative-share drop is real, though the '90%' dollar-for-dollar framing is shakier.
